statement: Effective January 15, 2026, BIS moved from a presumption of denial to case-by-case review of China export licenses for the NVIDIA H200, AMD MI325X and similar chips (below TPP 21,000 and 6,500 GB/s DRAM bandwidth), subject to security conditions, a roughly 25% tariff and a cap limiting China exports to under 50% of same-model US sales.
why: Loosening license policy reopens a licensed path for H200 and MI325X-class GPUs into China, partially expanding addressable demand.

impact.mechanism: Case-by-case licensing reopens some China GPU demand, but tariffs, security conditions and volume caps limit the upside.

evidence.source: U.S. Dept of Commerce, BIS press release
evidence.excerpt: BIS will now review export license applications for the Nvidia H200, AMD MI325X, and similar chips on a case-by-case basis provided certain security requirements are met

confidence.basis: Primary U.S. government (BIS) regulatory press release / Federal Register notice.
